description_long: This object consists of two vintage artillery cannons, each mounted on a pair of large spoked wheels and featuring elongated barrels. The cannons are constructed primarily from dark green metal, with one featuring a protective shield at the rear and a more angular, reinforced carriage, while the other has a simpler, open frame. Both carriages have towing hooks at the end, allowing them to be attached to vehicles or moved by hand. The wheels are robust and designed for traversing rough terrain, with visible axle and hub details. The barrels are long and cylindrical, with one slightly thicker and more reinforced than the other. The overall appearance suggests these are historical field artillery pieces, likely from the early to mid-20th century.
description: A pair of dark green, wheeled field artillery cannons with long barrels and spoked wheels, one featuring a protective shield and reinforced carriage.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full length of both cannons, highlighting the open carriage of one and the shielded, reinforced carriage of the other.
description_view_1: Rear angled view emphasizing the protective shield and the rear structure of the left cannon, as well as the open frame of the right cannon.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the length of the barrels and the spoked wheels, with the shielded cannon in the foreground.
description_view_3: Front angled view focusing on the towing hooks and the front ends of the carriages, with both barrels clearly visible.
attribute_weapon_mounts: Two fixed cannon barrels, each mounted centrally on their respective carriages; no additional weapon mounts visible.
attribute_armor_plating: One cannon features a flat, angled metal shield at the rear, providing partial frontal protection; the other has no visible armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: Solid dark green color; no camouflage pattern visible.
